methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series D → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.5x) to $1.4B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $500M - $9.2B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: Saronic
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Series D), post-money $9.25B

private.name: Chaos Industries
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Series D), post-money $4.5B

data.narrative:
Epirus, a Series D-stage defense and security company, has raised $550M+ to date and carries a current post-money valuation of $1.4B. The peer universe comprises 10 comparable companies—2 public (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private—spanning the same sector and stage band, including Saronic, Flock Safety, Chaos Industries, Skydio, and Hidden Level.

Two triangulation methods apply here. Stage-progression analysis, anchoring to the $1.4B last post-money and applying a typical 1.5x Series D step-up multiplier, yields $2.0B. Peer-median analysis across the 5-company sample produces $4.5B, with the full comparable set ranging from $500M to $9.2B. The disparity reflects meaningful stage and maturity variation within the peer cohort; Saronic and Flock Safety command $9.2B and $7.5B respectively at later stages, while Hidden Level at Series C registers $500M.

Triangulating these two methods produces a range of $1.7B to $5.2B, with a median of $3.3B. The current $1.4B valuation sits below the low end of this range, suggesting potential upside; however, the wide spread reflects uncertainty in peer comparability, particularly the stage heterogeneity within the comp set and the small sample size. Refinement would benefit from tighter stage alignment and deeper visibility into revenue multiples or growth profiles among peers.
